q: A force F acts at a distance r from the axis of rotation. If angle between F and r is 90°, torque is:
exp: Torque τ = rF sinθ. For θ = 90°, τ = Fr.

q: The SI unit of torque is equivalent to:
exp: Torque unit is N·m (not joule in rotational context).

q: Angular momentum of a particle is defined as:
exp: Angular momentum L = r × p.

q: If net external torque on a system is zero, then:
exp: Zero external torque implies conservation of angular momentum.

q: Moment of inertia depends on:
exp: It depends on how mass is distributed about axis.

q: A ring and a disc of same mass and radius roll down an incline. Which reaches first?
exp: Disc has smaller moment of inertia, so accelerates faster.

q: Moment of inertia of a thin rod about its center is:
exp: Standard result: I = (1/12)ML^2.

q: Angular velocity is defined as:
exp: Angular velocity ω = rate of change of angular displacement.

q: Rotational kinetic energy is given by:
A body rolls without slipping. Which condition is true?
exp: Rolling condition: v = ωr.

q: Angular acceleration is:
exp: Angular acceleration α = dω/dt.

q: Torque is maximum when angle between force and lever arm is:
exp: τ = rF sinθ is maximum at 90°.

q: The radius of gyration is defined as:
Angular momentum is analogous to:
exp: L = r × p is rotational analogue of linear momentum.

q: A solid sphere and hollow sphere have same mass and radius. Which has larger moment of inertia?
exp: Hollow sphere has more mass away from axis.

q: A torque produces:
exp: τ = Iα.

q: Work done in rotational motion is:
exp: Work = torque × angular displacement.

q: A body rotates with constant angular velocity. Net torque is:
exp: Constant ω implies zero angular acceleration.

q: Angular momentum of a rotating rigid body is:
exp: L = Iω.

q: If radius of a rotating disc is doubled keeping mass same, moment of inertia becomes:
exp: I ∝ r².

q: Which quantity is conserved in absence of external torque?
exp: Angular momentum remains constant if τ_ext = 0.

q: A skater pulls hands inward during spin to:
exp: L conserved ⇒ ω increases when I decreases.

q: The rotational analogue of mass is:
exp: Moment of inertia plays role similar to mass.

q: A force acting through center of mass produces:
exp: Force through CM does not create torque.

q: Angular momentum depends on:
exp: L = r × p depends on radius and momentum.

q: A rod is rotated about one end instead of center. Moment of inertia increases due to:
exp: I = I_cm + Md².

q: Angular displacement is a:
exp: It is treated as scalar in basic physics.

q: A torque τ acts on a body for time t. Angular impulse equals:
exp: Angular impulse = change in angular momentum = τt.

q: A disc rolls without slipping. Its total kinetic energy is:
Angular acceleration is zero when:
exp: α = τ/I.

q: Which body has least moment of inertia about same axis?
exp: Solid sphere has most mass near center.

q: If angular momentum doubles while I is constant, angular velocity becomes:
exp: L = Iω.

q: Torque has dimensions of:
In pure rolling motion, point of contact has velocity:
exp: Instantaneous velocity of contact point is zero.

q: Angular momentum is a:
exp: L = r × p is a vector quantity.

q: If no external torque acts, which quantity may still change?
exp: ω can change if I changes, but L remains constant.

q: A thin ring and solid disc have same mass and radius. Ratio of their moment of inertia is:
exp: Ring I = MR², disc I = (1/2)MR².

q: Angular velocity vector direction is given by:
exp: Right hand thumb rule.

q: A torque-free body in rotation will:
exp: No external torque ⇒ angular momentum conserved.

q: Rotational equilibrium requires:
opts: Both net force and net torque zero
exp: Both translational and rotational equilibrium conditions.

q: A body rotates faster when:
exp: Each condition can increase angular velocity depending on constraints.

q: A particle moves in circle and radius decreases. Angular velocity:
exp: If angular momentum conserved, ω increases when r decreases.

q: Work done by torque is maximum when:
exp: Work = τθ.
